content.full_text:
California 
Fair Political 
Practices Commission 
Muffie Meier 
1801 Broadway #101 
San Francisco, CA 
94109 
Dear Ms. Meier: 
May 14, 1986 
Re: 
Your Request for Advice 
Our File No. 1-86-122 
Thank you for your letter requesting confirmation on the 
new major donor disclosure requirements under the Political 
Reform Act. 
An individual or entity which makes contributions totaling 
$10,000 in a calendar year meets the definition of "committee" 
contained in Government Code Section 82013(c). 
These committees 
are referred to as "major donor committees". 
Once an individual or entity has qualified as a major donor 
committee it must file a semi-annual statement for each half of 
the year during which it makes any contributions. 
Semi-annual 
statements are due on July 31, 1986, and January 31, 1987, with 
closing dates of June 30 and December 31, respectively. 
"City" 
major donor committees (i.e., committees which contribute to 
candidates or measures within a single city during an election 
year) are required to file pre-election statements. 
However, 
"State" and "County" major donor committees are not required to 
file pre-election statements. 
A "State" major donor committee which also makes 
contributions to or expenditures on behalf of city or county 
candidates or measures is not required to file reports as a 
"City" or "County" major donor committee. 
However, if a 
committee is initially required to file as a "City" or "County" 
major donor committee and later during the calendar year it 
qualifies as another type of committee, it must continue to file 
copies of its campaign statements in the original city or county 
(in addition to the other locations in which it is required to 
file) until the end of the calendar year. 
428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660 


Ms. Meier 
May 14, 1986 
Page Two 
Enclosed is a copy of the revised 1986 Campaign Manual 
which provides additional information for major donors. 
If you have any further questions, please feel free to call 
me at (916) 322-5662. 
Enclosure 
AH:cah 
Yours truly, 
Alice Hughes 
Technical Assistance and Analysis 


California 
Fair Political 
Practices Commission 
April 17, 1986 
Muffie Meier 
1801 Broadway #101 
San Francisco, CA 
94109 
Re: 
86-122 
Dear Ms. Meier: 
Your letter requesting advice under the Political Reform 
Act has been received by the Fair Political Practices 
Commission. 
If you have any questions about your advice 
request, you may contact the Technical Assistance and Analysis 
Division at (916 ) 322 -5662. 
We try to answer all advice requests promptly. 
Therefore, 
unless your request poses particularly complex legal questions, 
or unless more information is needed to answer your request, 
you should expect a response within 21 working days. 
JP:plh 
Very truly y~urs, 
tt a~"LL 9u);:,-1!c,v J fo ;yJt_ 
Jeanne Pritchard 
~ 
Chief 
Technical Assistance and Analysis 
Division 
428 J Street, Suite 800 • 
P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804~0807 • 
(916) 322~C;660 


3 April 1986 
Ms. Alice Hughes 
Fair Political Practices Commission 
Technical Assistance 
P.O. Box 807 
Sacramento, California 
95804-0807 
Dear Ms. Hughes: 
This is to confirm our conversation during the week of 
10 March 1986. 
I am in receipt of FPPC form 461 for 1986, Fact Sheet F 
dated January 1986, and Supplemental Campaign Disclosure 
Information booklet dated February 1986. 
After reviewing 
the above and speaking with you it is my understanding that 
a major donor files semi-annually when the donor donates 
$10,000.00 in any combination of committees. 
The donor 
must file threshold statements when the donor exceeds 
$10,000.00 in either a city or a county combination of 
committees but not if any state committees are included 
even if $10,000.00 is exceeded in either the city or county 
and any amount over $10,000.00 is from state committees. 
(Example: 
during threshold one a total of $10,000.00 in 
three county races is contributed and $2,000.00 to one 
state race -- a semi-annual statement must be filed but not 
a threshold) 
Please let me know if the above meets with the new requirements 
or if it does not how I need to adjust. 
Thank you. 
Meier

sections.question: a major donor files semi-annually when the donor donates $10,000.00 in any combination of committees. The donor must file threshold statements when the donor exceeds $10,000.00 in either a city or a county combination of committees but not if any state committees are included even if $10,000.00 is exceeded in either the city or county and any amount over $10,000.00 is from state committees.
sections.conclusion: An individual or entity which makes contributions totaling $10,000 in a calendar year meets the definition of 'committee' and must file semi-annual statements for each half of the year during which contributions are made. 'City' major donor committees are required to file pre-election statements, but 'State' and 'County' committees are not. A 'State' major donor committee making contributions to city or county candidates/measures is not required to file as a 'City' or 'County' committee.
sections.question_synthetic: Ms. Meier seeks confirmation of major donor disclosure requirements under the Political Reform Act, specifically regarding when semi-annual and threshold statements must be filed based on contribution amounts and types of committees.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC confirms that major donors making $10,000 in contributions must file semi-annual statements (due July 31, 1986 and January 31, 1987), with filing requirements varying by committee type. State-level major donor committees are exempt from pre-election statement filing requirements even if they also contribute to city or county races.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document contains both incoming request (dated April 3, 1986) and FPPC response letters (dated April 17 and May 14, 1986). The May 14 response is the primary advice letter. Question text extracted from incoming request; conclusion synthesized from main response letter body.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: a major donor files semi-annually when the donor donates $10,000.00 in any combination of committees. The donor must file threshold statements when the donor exceeds $10,000.00 in either a city or a county combination of committees but not if any state committees are included even if $10,000.00 is exceeded in either the city or county and any amount over $10,000.00 is from state committees.

CONCLUSION: An individual or entity which makes contributions totaling $10,000 in a calendar year meets the definition of 'committee' and must file semi-annual statements for each half of the year during which contributions are made. 'City' major donor committees are required to file pre-election statements, but 'State' and 'County' committees are not. A 'State' major donor committee making contributions to city or county candidates/measures is not required to file as a 'City' or 'County' committee.

embedding.summary: Ms. Meier requested FPPC advice on major donor disclosure requirements under the Political Reform Act. The FPPC responded with guidance on the $10,000 threshold for 'major donor committee' status, semi-annual filing deadlines, and differential pre-election statement requirements based on committee type.
